---
title: "Presentation title"
subtitle: "A short, factual subtitle"
author: "Author Name, Role"
date: today
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
format: probity-pptx-pptx
---

# Part one: framing

## A content slide

Content slides carry the small navy logo, a hairline rule, a navy title, and
the Probity wordmark in the footer. Keep one idea per slide.

- Lead with the answer, then the qualification
- Short bullets, sentence case, no terminal full stops on fragments
- UK spelling throughout (analyse, behaviour, recognised)

## Two columns

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Left column.** Use columns for before/after or claim/evidence pairs. The
navy lead-in phrase followed by a full stop is the Probity inline-emphasis
pattern.
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Right column.** State sample sizes and limitations openly. Do not bury
caveats.
:::
:::

## A table

| Variable        | Lag   | Correlation |
|-----------------|-------|-------------|
| GDP growth      | lag-1 | -0.93       |
| Unemployment    | lag-1 | -0.41       |
| Household debt  | lag-0 | 0.62        |

: Loss-rate drivers, FY 2019/20 to FY 2023/24 {tbl-colwidths="[40,20,40]"}

## Headline numbers

[[statcards]]

- Sample :: 6 :: Fiscal-year observations the model is fitted on
- Fit :: 0.86 :: R-squared on lag-1 GDP, before the COVID-year check
- *Multiplier :: R 14.9M :: Applied forward-looking provision uplift

## Three steps

[[cards]]

- Estimate :: Fit the univariate model on the six fiscal-year observations and record the sign against theory.
- Stress :: Drop the FY 2020/21 anomaly and refit. Report how far the fit moves.
- Apply :: Scale every cell of the provision matrix by the single multiplier alpha.

## A native chart

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Provision multiplier by fiscal year"
library(ggplot2)
df <- data.frame(
  year = c("2019/20", "2020/21", "2021/22", "2022/23"),
  alpha = c(4.40, 20.10, 10.64, 7.85)
)
ggplot(df, aes(year, alpha)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#0A325A", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", alpha)), vjust = -0.4,
            colour = "#0A325A", fontface = "bold") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Calibri") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "#D5DEE9", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "#6B7280")
  )
```

# Thank you
